CF1000.requirement: When CF receives a "No-Op" command, CF shall increment the "CF Valid-Command Counter" and issue a cFE event message.

matches.name: CfdpCommands
matches.ai_generated_desc: This interface defines all ground commands accepted by the application to control file transfers and configure operations. It includes commands for initiating transfers, freezing channels, suspending transactions, and managing queues.

matches.name: EventId
matches.ai_generated_desc: This status represents the software bus event identifiers used by the application to log informational, debugging, or error messages. It covers events from initialization, protocol data unit processing, engine operations, and command execution.

matches.name: NoopCmd
matches.ai_generated_desc: A command that performs no operation other than incrementing the command execution counter. It is primarily used to verify that the Table Services application is alive and receiving commands.

matches.name: HKCommandCounters
matches.ai_generated_desc: Tracks the number of commands successfully processed and the number of commands that resulted in an error. This helps operators verify command execution health.

CF1001.requirement: When a "Reset-Counters" command is received, CF shall set the command-specified "housekeeping counters" to a value of zero.

matches.name: Reset
matches.ai_generated_desc: Specifies the target category of counters to clear when executing a reset command. This allows operators to reset specific statistics such as command errors, faults, or directional transfer counts.

matches.name: ResetCountersCmd
matches.ai_generated_desc: A command that resets the Table Services command execution and command error counters back to zero. This is typically used to clear historical statistics during testing or nominal operations.

matches.name: CfdpCommands
matches.ai_generated_desc: This interface defines all ground commands accepted by the application to control file transfers and configure operations. It includes commands for initiating transfers, freezing channels, suspending transactions, and managing queues.

matches.name: HkFault
matches.ai_generated_desc: Housekeeping counters for various faults and errors encountered during file operations and protocol execution. It tracks file system errors, cyclic redundancy check mismatches, and protocol timer expirations.

matches.name: HKCommandCounters
matches.ai_generated_desc: Tracks the number of commands successfully processed and the number of commands that resulted in an error. This helps operators verify command execution health.

matches.name: HkRecv
matches.ai_generated_desc: Housekeeping counters for received data. It tracks received file data bytes, protocol data units, errors, spurious or dropped packets, and negative acknowledgment segment requests.

CF1002.requirement: If a command's header-specified length is not equal to the expected command length, CF shall reject the command.

matches.name: CfdpCommands
matches.ai_generated_desc: This interface defines all ground commands accepted by the application to control file transfers and configure operations. It includes commands for initiating transfers, freezing channels, suspending transactions, and managing queues.

matches.name: EventId
matches.ai_generated_desc: This status represents the software bus event identifiers used by the application to log informational, debugging, or error messages. It covers events from initialization, protocol data unit processing, engine operations, and command execution.

matches.name: CmdSecHdr
matches.ai_generated_desc: Represents the secondary header for commands, which contains the specific function code identifying the operation to be executed. It is used to route and validate incoming commands.

CF2000.requirement: When CF receives a CFDP receive-file protocol-directive PDU, CF shall process the file transfer request according to the directive-specified CFDP Mode, "Unacknowledged" Class-1 Service or "Acknowledged" Class-2 Service.

matches.name: CfdpPduIO
matches.ai_generated_desc: This interface handles the reception and transmission of raw protocol data unit buffers between the software bus and the protocol engine. It serves as the primary data path for incoming and outgoing file transfer packets.

matches.name: CFDP
matches.ai_generated_desc: Specifies the CCSDS File Delivery Protocol class of service used for a transaction. Class 1 represents unreliable transfer without acknowledgments, while Class 2 represents reliable transfer using negative acknowledgments.

matches.name: FileDirective
matches.ai_generated_desc: This status represents the specific file directive code carried within a CCSDS File Delivery Protocol protocol data unit. It identifies the control message type, such as End-of-File, Finished, Acknowledgment, Metadata, or Negative Acknowledgment.

matches.name: RxSubState
matches.ai_generated_desc: This status represents the sub-state of an active receive transaction. It tracks progress through receiving data, requesting missing segments via negative acknowledgments, validating file cyclic redundancy checks, performing filestore operations, or completing the final handshake.

matches.name: TxnState
matches.ai_generated_desc: This status represents the high-level state of a CCSDS File Delivery Protocol transaction. It tracks whether a transaction is unused, newly initialized, actively sending or receiving Class 1 or Class 2 files, or held after completion.

CF2001.requirement: CF shall extract uplinked CFDP PDUs from cFE-SB messages.

matches.name: CfdpPduIO
matches.ai_generated_desc: This interface handles the reception and transmission of raw protocol data unit buffers between the software bus and the protocol engine. It serves as the primary data path for incoming and outgoing file transfer packets.

matches.name: Message
matches.ai_generated_desc: Represents a basic software bus message containing a standard Consultative Committee for Space Data Systems space packet header. It serves as the foundation for communication within the flight software.

matches.name: SoftwareBusAccess
matches.ai_generated_desc: Provides access to the software bus for a specific message identifier, supporting synchronous sending and receiving of messages alongside asynchronous reading of the message identifier.

CF2002.requirement: CF shall extract file data from File-Data PDUs and reconstruct an identical copy of the extracted file from the meta-data-specified directory.

matches.name: TxnFilenames
matches.ai_generated_desc: Stores the source and destination file paths for a specific file transfer transaction. This is used to track where a file is being read from and written to during a transfer.

matches.name: EotPacket_Payload
matches.ai_generated_desc: Contains the detailed status and metadata of a completed file transfer transaction at its end of transaction. This includes transaction state, file size, cyclic redundancy check results, entity identifiers, and file paths.

matches.name: CfdpPduIO
matches.ai_generated_desc: This interface handles the reception and transmission of raw protocol data unit buffers between the software bus and the protocol engine. It serves as the primary data path for incoming and outgoing file transfer packets.

CF2002.1.requirement: If CF detects that a "fault" has occurred, CF shall close out the transaction and issue an "Error" cFE event message.

matches.name: TxnStatus
matches.ai_generated_desc: This status represents the final outcome of a transaction, combining standard CCSDS File Delivery Protocol condition codes with local application-specific fault codes. It provides detailed diagnostic information about why a transaction succeeded, failed, or was aborted.

matches.name: EventId
matches.ai_generated_desc: This status represents the software bus event identifiers used by the application to log informational, debugging, or error messages. It covers events from initialization, protocol data unit processing, engine operations, and command execution.

CF2002.1.2.requirement:
CF shall detect the following scenarios and identify them as faults:
1. Positive Ack Limit Reached
2. Filestore Rejection
3. File-CRC Mismatch Failure
4. File-Size Error
5. NAK Limit Reached
6. Inactivity Limit Reached

matches.name: TxnStatus

matches.name: ConditionCode

matches.name: ConfigTable
matches.ai_generated_desc: The main configuration table for the file transfer application. It contains global settings such as the local entity identifier, timing parameters, channel configurations, and directories for temporary or failed files.

CF4000.1.requirement: CF-channel parameters shall be defined by a CF configuration table.

matches.name: ChannelConfig
matches.ai_generated_desc: Defines the configuration parameters for a specific communication channel. This includes message limits, timers, limits for acknowledgments and negative acknowledgments, software bus message identifiers, and directory paths.

matches.name: ConfigTable
matches.ai_generated_desc: The main configuration table for the file transfer application. It contains global settings such as the local entity identifier, timing parameters, channel configurations, and directories for temporary or failed files.

CF4000.1.1.requirement:
The "CF Configuration Table" shall include the following parameters:
-- Engine parameter(s)
1. Local entity ID
-- Channel parameters (per-channel)
2. Dequeue Enable 
3. Transmit PDU MID
4. Receive PDU MID
5. Handshake Semaphore Name
6. Acknowledge timer limit
7. Non-acknowledge timer limit
8. Acknowledge retry limit
9. Non-acknowledge retry limit
10. Inactivity timer limit
-- Polling Directory Parameters (per-polling directory)
11. Enable State
12. CFDP Class
13. Priority
14. Source Path
15. Destination Path
16. Peer Entity Id

matches.name: ChannelConfig
matches.ai_generated_desc: Defines the configuration parameters for a specific communication channel. This includes message limits, timers, limits for acknowledgments and negative acknowledgments, software bus message identifiers, and directory paths.

matches.name: ConfigTable
matches.ai_generated_desc: The main configuration table for the file transfer application. It contains global settings such as the local entity identifier, timing parameters, channel configurations, and directories for temporary or failed files.

CF4000.2.requirement: Each CF channel shall have a dedicated and independent pending queue.

matches.name: QueueIdx
matches.ai_generated_desc: Identifies the internal transaction queues used to manage the lifecycle of file transfers. These queues track pending, transmitting, receiving, and historical transactions.

matches.name: Queue
matches.ai_generated_desc: Represents the operational state categories of transaction queues. It is used to target specific groups of transactions, such as pending, active, or completed history, during commands or queries.

CF4001.requirement: When CF receives an "Enable Polling" command, CF shall enable poll-directory processing.

matches.name: EnableDirPollingCmd
matches.ai_generated_desc: This command enables automatic directory polling for a specified channel and directory index. It allows the application to periodically scan the directory and automatically initiate file transfers for newly found files.

matches.name: PollDir
matches.ai_generated_desc: Configuration for a directory that is periodically scanned for new files to transfer automatically. It defines the polling interval, priority, destination entity identifier, source and destination directories, and whether the polling is active.

CF4001.2.1.requirement: CF shall send all files in the polling directory at the configuration-table-specified priority level.

matches.name: PollDir

matches.name: DisableDirPollingCmd

CF5002.requirement:
The following parameters shall be configurable by a CF command:
1. CFDP channel Ack-Timer Value (seconds)
2. CFDP channel NAK-Timer Value (seconds)  
3. CFDP channel Inactivity-Timeout Value (seconds) 
4. CFDP channel Maximum Ack Timeouts (integer counts)
5. CFDP channel Maximum NAK Timeouts (integer counts)

CF5002.1.requirement:
The default values for the CFDP channel protocol parameters below shall be defined by a CF Configuration Table:
1. CFDP channel Ack-Timer Value (seconds)
2. CFDP channel NAK-Timer Value (seconds)  
3. CFDP channel Inactivity-Timeout Value (seconds) 
4. CFDP channel Maximum Ack Timeouts (integer counts)
5. CFDP channel Maximum NAK Timeouts (integer counts)

CF6000.requirement:
The CF Housekeeping message shall include
1. Command Counter
2. Command Error Counter
-- Per-Channel HouseKeeping Entries
4. Number of transactions on the various queues
5. Frozen Status
6. Number of Valid PDUs Received
7. Number of Receive-Transaction Errors
8. Number of Sent PDUs 
9. Number of Acknowledge Retry Limit Exceeded Faults
10. Number of Non-Acknowledge Retry Limit Exceeded Faults
11. Number of Inactivity Timer-Limit Faults
12. Number of CRC Mismatch Faults
13. Number of Filestore Rejection Faults (can be split by type)
14. Number of Filesize Mismatch Faults

matches.name: HkFault
matches.ai_generated_desc: Housekeeping counters for various faults and errors encountered during file operations and protocol execution. It tracks file system errors, cyclic redundancy check mismatches, and protocol timer expirations.

matches.name: HousekeepingTlm_Payload
matches.ai_generated_desc: Contains the housekeeping telemetry data for the software bus task, tracking command execution, communication errors, and memory pool usage. It is used by operators to monitor the operational health of the software bus.

matches.name: HkChannel_Data
matches.ai_generated_desc: Housekeeping data specific to an individual communication channel. It includes channel-specific counters, queue sizes, polling and playback activity, and whether the channel is currently frozen.

matches.name: HkCounters
matches.ai_generated_desc: A consolidated set of housekeeping counters grouping transmission, reception, and fault statistics. It provides a comprehensive overview of the application's operational performance.

extracted_variables.name: Number of transactions on the various queues
Number of Acknowledge Retry Limit Exceeded Faults
Number of Non-Acknowledge Retry Limit Exceeded Faults

CF6001.requirement:
The CF End of Transaction message shall include
1. Transaction identifier
2. Channel number
3. Direction of transaction
4. Filenames associated with transaction
5. Transaction state
6. Condition code
7. Source EID of transaction
8. Peer EID of transaction
9. File size
10. CRC state

matches.name: TxnStatus
matches.ai_generated_desc: This status represents the final outcome of a transaction, combining standard CCSDS File Delivery Protocol condition codes with local application-specific fault codes. It provides detailed diagnostic information about why a transaction succeeded, failed, or was aborted.

matches.name: EotPacket_Payload
matches.ai_generated_desc: Contains the detailed status and metadata of a completed file transfer transaction at its end of transaction. This includes transaction state, file size, cyclic redundancy check results, entity identifiers, and file paths.

matches.name: ConditionCode
matches.ai_generated_desc: This status represents standard CCSDS File Delivery Protocol condition codes carried in control messages to report transaction results or errors. It indicates conditions like successful completion, file checksum failures, inactivity timeouts, or cancel requests.

matches.name: TxnState
matches.ai_generated_desc: This status represents the high-level state of a CCSDS File Delivery Protocol transaction. It tracks whether a transaction is unused, newly initialized, actively sending or receiving Class 1 or Class 2 files, or held after completion.

CF6001.1.requirement: When a transaction terminates, CF shall generate an End of Transaction information packet

matches.name: EotPacket_Payload
matches.ai_generated_desc: Contains the detailed status and metadata of a completed file transfer transaction at its end of transaction. This includes transaction state, file size, cyclic redundancy check results, entity identifiers, and file paths.

CF6001.2.requirement: If a transaction has reached the FIN or EOF state, the CF End of Transaction message Condition Code shall be set to the CFDP condition code for that transaction.

matches.name: ConditionCode
matches.ai_generated_desc: This status represents standard CCSDS File Delivery Protocol condition codes carried in control messages to report transaction results or errors. It indicates conditions like successful completion, file checksum failures, inactivity timeouts, or cancel requests.

matches.name: FileDirective
matches.ai_generated_desc: This status represents the specific file directive code carried within a CCSDS File Delivery Protocol protocol data unit. It identifies the control message type, such as End-of-File, Finished, Acknowledgment, Metadata, or Negative Acknowledgment.

matches.name: TxSubState
matches.ai_generated_desc: This status represents the sub-state of an active send transaction. It tracks progress through sending metadata, transmitting file data, performing local filestore operations, or completing the transfer.

matches.name: RxSubState
matches.ai_generated_desc: This status represents the sub-state of an active receive transaction. It tracks progress through receiving data, requesting missing segments via negative acknowledgments, validating file cyclic redundancy checks, performing filestore operations, or completing the final handshake.

matches.name: EotPacket_Payload
matches.ai_generated_desc: Contains the detailed status and metadata of a completed file transfer transaction at its end of transaction. This includes transaction state, file size, cyclic redundancy check results, entity identifiers, and file paths.

extracted_variables.name: CF End of Transaction message Condition Code

CF6001.3.requirement: If the transaction has not reached the FIN or EOF state, the CF End of Transaction message Condition Code shall indicate why the transaction stopped early.

CF7000.requirement: When CF is initialized, CF shall initialize the the housekeeping counters/numbers to zero and the frozen status as "thawed".

matches.name: CfdpCommands
matches.ai_generated_desc: This interface defines all ground commands accepted by the application to control file transfers and configure operations. It includes commands for initiating transfers, freezing channels, suspending transactions, and managing queues.

CF7001.requirement: When CF is initialized, CF shall load valid CF Configuration Tables.

matches.name: EventId
matches.ai_generated_desc: This status represents the software bus event identifiers used by the application to log informational, debugging, or error messages. It covers events from initialization, protocol data unit processing, engine operations, and command execution.

matches.name: ConfigTable
matches.ai_generated_desc: The main configuration table for the file transfer application. It contains global settings such as the local entity identifier, timing parameters, channel configurations, and directories for temporary or failed files.

CF7001.1.requirement: If a CF Configuration Table fails validation, CF shall issue an error cFE event message and exit.

matches.name: EventId
matches.ai_generated_desc: This status represents the software bus event identifiers used by the application to log informational, debugging, or error messages. It covers events from initialization, protocol data unit processing, engine operations, and command execution.

CF7001.1.1.requirement: CF shall validate CF configuration table parameters.

matches.name: ConfigTable
matches.ai_generated_desc: The main configuration table for the file transfer application. It contains global settings such as the local entity identifier, timing parameters, channel configurations, and directories for temporary or failed files.

matches.name: EventId
matches.ai_generated_desc: This status represents the software bus event identifiers used by the application to log informational, debugging, or error messages. It covers events from initialization, protocol data unit processing, engine operations, and command execution.

matches.name: ChannelConfig
matches.ai_generated_desc: Defines the configuration parameters for a specific communication channel. This includes message limits, timers, limits for acknowledgments and negative acknowledgments, software bus message identifiers, and directory paths.

CF8000.requirement: When CF receives an "Initialize Engine" command, CF shall enable the CFDP engine and re-load its configuration.

matches.name: CfdpCommands
matches.ai_generated_desc: This interface defines all ground commands accepted by the application to control file transfers and configure operations. It includes commands for initiating transfers, freezing channels, suspending transactions, and managing queues.

matches.name: EnableEngineCmd
matches.ai_generated_desc: This command reinitializes and enables the core CCSDS File Delivery Protocol engine. It resumes all file transfer processing and protocol state machines.

CF8001.requirement: When CF receives a "Disable Engine" command, CF shall disable the CFDP engine and reset its state.

matches.name: CfdpCommands
matches.ai_generated_desc: This interface defines all ground commands accepted by the application to control file transfers and configure operations. It includes commands for initiating transfers, freezing channels, suspending transactions, and managing queues.

matches.name: DisableEngineCmd
matches.ai_generated_desc: This command disables the core CCSDS File Delivery Protocol engine. It halts all file transfer processing, typically to allow safe updates to configuration tables.
